record.notes: Correctly states the hypothesis: G is a finite group of prime order p. Notes that p>=2 so a non-identity element exists.
Correctly considers the cyclic subgroup generated by the non-identity element g.
Correctly applies Lagrange's theorem: |<g>| divides |G| = p.
Correctly reasons that since p is prime, its only divisors are 1 and p. Since g != e, |<g>| >= 2, so |<g>| != 1, hence |<g>| = p.
Correctly concludes <g> = G since |<g>| = |G| and <g> ⊆ G.
Correctly concludes G is cyclic as it is generated by g.
All assumptions are used: finiteness (to apply Lagrange), prime order (to use prime divisor argument). No unused assumptions.
